title: Fixing Incorrect Type Inference for a Generic Function with Union Types

statement.markdown: Implement a generic function `isEven` that takes a number `n` and returns a boolean indicating whether `n` is even. The function should work for both integers and floating-point numbers. However, the current implementation has a logical bug that affects type inference. Fix the bug to make the function work correctly for all types of numbers.
statement.inputFormat: The function should accept a single argument `n` of any type.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return a boolean value indicating whether `n` is even.
statement.notes: The function should work for both positive and negative numbers, as well as zero.

examples.explanation: The function should return true for even numbers.
The function should return false for odd numbers.
The function should return false for non-integer numbers.

hints.text: Check the type annotations and ensure they are correct
Consider using a type guard to narrow the type of the input

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The key insight is to use a type guard to narrow the type of the input, allowing the type inference to work correctly.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute-force approach would involve using a simple conditional statement to determine whether the number is even.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would involve using a type guard to narrow the type of the input, allowing the type inference to work correctly.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using a type guard to narrow the type of the input, allowing the type inference to work correctly. The type guard is implemented using a function that takes a number and returns a boolean indicating whether the number is even.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by demonstrating that the type guard correctly narrows the type of the input.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the solution involves plugging in example inputs to verify that the function works correctly.
commonMistakes: Failure to use a type guard
